basics.description:
## SpyCloud for Microsoft Sentinel

![SpyCloud](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iammrherb/SPYCLOUD-SENTINEL/main/docs/images/spycloud-wordmark-black.png)

**Version 11.0 -- Enterprise Identity Threat Protection**

Deploy automated identity threat detection and response powered by SpyCloud's recaptured darknet intelligence, fully integrated into Microsoft Sentinel.

[SpyCloud Portal](https://portal.spycloud.com) | [Documentation](https://github.com/iammrherb/SPYCLOUD-SENTINEL)

options.text: SpyCloud for Microsoft Sentinel delivers automated identity threat protection by integrating SpyCloud's recaptured darknet intelligence directly into your security operations workflow.

Select the Azure region for your deployment. Microsoft Sentinel and all required resource providers must be available in the chosen region. If you have an existing Log Analytics workspace, select the same region where it resides.

Select the deployment scope. Full Deployment is recommended for new installations. Upgrade mode adds new components while preserving existing resources. Selective modes deploy only the specified component category.

elements.name: deploymentMode
elements.label: Deployment Mode *
elements.toolTip: Determines which components are deployed.

elements.name: workspaceMode
elements.label: Workspace
elements.toolTip: Choose whether to create a new workspace or use an existing one.

elements.name: newWorkspaceName
elements.label: New Workspace Name *
elements.toolTip: Name for the new Log Analytics workspace. Must be globally unique, 4-63 characters, and contain only letters, numbers, and hyphens.

constraints.validationMessage: Workspace name must be 4-63 characters, start with a letter, and contain only letters, numbers, and hyphens.

elements.name: existingWorkspaceSelector
elements.label: Existing Workspace *
elements.toolTip: Select the Log Analytics workspace with Microsoft Sentinel enabled.

elements.name: workspaceSku
elements.label: Workspace Pricing Tier
elements.toolTip: Select the pricing tier for the Log Analytics workspace.

elements.name: dataRetention
elements.label: Data Retention Period
elements.toolTip: Number of days to retain data in the workspace. Longer retention enables historical investigations but increases storage costs.

options.text: Select the SpyCloud products you are licensed for and provide the corresponding API key for each. Each product uses its own API key from portal.spycloud.com > Settings > API Keys. Enterprise/Compass share a single key. All other products require their own dedicated key. If you select Enterprise/Compass, all data source options will be available.
You must enable at least one product below. Only enable products you are licensed for — using an invalid or unauthorized key will cause 403 Forbidden errors during data ingestion.

elements.name: enableEnterprise
elements.label: Enterprise / Compass — Breach watchlist monitoring, breach catalog, identity exposure, and Compass infostealer intelligence. Selecting this product enables ALL data source pollers below.
elements.toolTip: Enable if you have a SpyCloud Enterprise or Enterprise+ subscription. Enterprise and Compass share the same API key.

elements.name: enterpriseApiKey
elements.toolTip: Your SpyCloud Enterprise/Compass API key from portal.spycloud.com. This single key covers Enterprise breach data, Compass infostealer data, and identity exposure endpoints.

constraints.validationMessage: API key must be at least 8 characters.

options.text: With Enterprise/Compass enabled, all data source pollers (Breach Watchlist, Breach Catalog, Compass, SIP, Investigations, IDLink, Exposure, CAP, Data Partnership) can be individually enabled below. The Enterprise key is used as the default for any poller that does not have a dedicated product key.

elements.name: monitoredDomain
elements.label: Monitored Domain *
elements.toolTip: Your primary corporate domain to monitor across all SpyCloud APIs (e.g., example.com). Required for Investigations, IDLINK, Data Partnership, Exposure, CAP, and SIP connectors.

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid domain name (e.g., example.com).

options.text: ── Standalone Products (each requires its own dedicated API key) ──

elements.name: enableSipProduct
elements.label: Session Identity Protection (SIP) — Stolen session cookies and tokens for your monitored domains.
elements.toolTip: Enable if you have a standalone SIP subscription or SIP entitlement with a dedicated key.

elements.name: sipProductApiKey
elements.toolTip: Your dedicated SIP API key from portal.spycloud.com.

constraints.validationMessage: API key must be at least 8 characters.

elements.name: sipProductApiKeyOptional

label.password: SIP API Key (optional — leave blank to use Enterprise key)

elements.toolTip: Optional dedicated SIP API key. Leave blank to use your Enterprise/Compass key for SIP endpoints.

elements.name: enableInvestigationsProduct
elements.label: Investigations — Deep-dive investigative data for threat hunting and incident response across the full SpyCloud dataset.
elements.toolTip: Enable if you have a standalone Investigations subscription or Investigations entitlement with a dedicated key.

elements.name: investigationsProductApiKey
elements.toolTip: Your dedicated Investigations API key from portal.spycloud.com.

constraints.validationMessage: API key must be at least 8 characters.

elements.name: investigationsProductApiKeyOptional

label.password: Investigations API Key (optional — leave blank to use Enterprise key)

elements.toolTip: Optional dedicated Investigations API key. Leave blank to use your Enterprise/Compass key.

elements.name: enableIdLinkProduct
elements.label: IDLink — Identity correlation and graph analysis mapping relationships between compromised identities.
elements.toolTip: Enable if you have a standalone IDLink subscription with a dedicated API key.

elements.name: idlinkProductApiKey
elements.toolTip: Your dedicated IDLink API key from portal.spycloud.com.

constraints.validationMessage: API key must be at least 8 characters.

elements.name: enableDataPartnershipProduct
elements.label: Data Partnership — Partner-sourced intelligence feeds for expanded compromised credential coverage.
elements.toolTip: Enable if you have a Data Partnership subscription with a dedicated API key.

elements.name: dataPartnershipProductApiKey
elements.toolTip: Your dedicated Data Partnership API key from portal.spycloud.com.

constraints.validationMessage: API key must be at least 8 characters.

elements.name: enableExposureProduct
elements.label: Exposure — Aggregated exposure metrics and risk scores for monitored assets.
elements.toolTip: Enable if you have an Exposure subscription with a dedicated API key.

elements.name: exposureProductApiKey
elements.toolTip: Your dedicated Exposure API key from portal.spycloud.com.

constraints.validationMessage: API key must be at least 8 characters.

elements.name: enableCapProduct
elements.label: CAP (Credential Access Program) — Automated credential protection policies and lifecycle management.
elements.toolTip: Enable if you have a CAP subscription with a dedicated API key.

elements.name: capProductApiKey
elements.toolTip: Your dedicated CAP API key from portal.spycloud.com.

constraints.validationMessage: API key must be at least 8 characters.

options.text: ── Polling Configuration ──

elements.name: pollingInterval
elements.label: Polling Interval
elements.toolTip: How frequently the data connector retrieves new records from SpyCloud APIs.

elements.name: dceName
elements.label: Data Collection Endpoint Name
elements.toolTip: Name for the Data Collection Endpoint resource.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be 3-64 characters, start with a letter, and contain only letters, numbers, and hyphens.

elements.name: dcrName
elements.label: Data Collection Rule Name
elements.toolTip: Name for the Data Collection Rule resource.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be 3-64 characters, start with a letter, and contain only letters, numbers, and hyphens.

options.text: Enable the API pollers for data sources included in your SpyCloud subscription. Each poller creates a dedicated custom log table in your workspace. Disable any data sources not covered by your API tier to avoid ingestion errors.
No SpyCloud products are enabled. You must enable at least one product in the API Configuration section above to deploy data pollers.

elements.name: enableBreachWatchlist
elements.label: Breach Watchlist — Monitors your organization's domains and email addresses against SpyCloud's breach database for compromised credentials and PII. (Requires Enterprise/Compass)
elements.toolTip: Enables the Breach Watchlist data poller. Requires Enterprise/Compass product.

elements.name: enableBreachCatalog
elements.label: Breach Catalog — Provides metadata about breaches in SpyCloud's database including source, severity, acquisition date, and affected record counts. (Requires Enterprise/Compass)
elements.toolTip: Enables the Breach Catalog data poller. Requires Enterprise/Compass product.

elements.name: enableCompass
elements.label: Compass — Delivers infostealer malware intelligence including infected device details, stolen credentials, and session cookies. (Requires Enterprise/Compass)
elements.toolTip: Enables the Compass data poller. Requires Enterprise/Compass product.

elements.name: enableSIP
elements.label: Session Identity Protection (SIP) — Detects stolen session cookies and tokens for your monitored domains.
elements.toolTip: Enables the SIP data poller.

elements.name: sipCookieDomain
elements.label: SIP Cookie Domain *
elements.toolTip: The cookie domain to monitor for stolen session cookies (e.g., 'example.com').

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid domain (e.g., example.com).

elements.name: enableInvestigations
elements.label: Investigations — Deep-dive investigative data for threat hunting and incident response across the full SpyCloud dataset.
elements.toolTip: Enables the Investigations data poller.

elements.name: enableIDLink
elements.label: IDLink — Maps relationships between compromised identities, devices, and credentials to reveal identity linkage patterns.
elements.toolTip: Enables the IDLink data poller.

elements.name: enableExposure
elements.label: Exposure — Aggregates exposure metrics and risk scores for monitored assets based on historical and ongoing breach data.
elements.toolTip: Enables the Exposure data poller.

elements.name: enableCAP
elements.label: Compromised Application Protection (CAP) — Identifies compromised consumer accounts and application credentials in real time.
elements.toolTip: Enables the CAP data poller.

elements.name: enableDataPartnership
elements.label: Data Partnership — Ingests partner-sourced intelligence feeds for expanded coverage of compromised credential sources.
elements.toolTip: Enables the Data Partnership data poller.

options.text: All analytics rules deploy as TEMPLATES. No rules are created in an active or enabled state. After deployment, navigate to Sentinel > Analytics > Rule Templates to review and selectively enable rules appropriate for your environment.

elements.name: enableCoreDetectionRules
elements.label: Enable Core Detection Rules -- Foundational rules for detecting compromised credentials, new breach exposures, high-severity records, password reuse, and freshly recaptured data from SpyCloud watchlist monitoring.
elements.toolTip: Deploys core detection rule templates for SpyCloud breach and credential intelligence.

elements.name: sectionO365Entra
elements.label: O365 and Entra ID Correlation Rules

elements.name: enableO365EntraRules
elements.label: Enable O365/Entra Correlation Rules -- Correlates SpyCloud breach data with Azure AD sign-in logs, Office 365 audit logs, and Entra ID events to detect credential misuse in your Microsoft environment.
elements.toolTip: Deploys rule templates that cross-reference SpyCloud data with Microsoft 365 and Entra ID telemetry.

elements.name: enableUEBANetworkRules
elements.label: Enable UEBA/Network Rules -- Combines User and Entity Behavior Analytics with SpyCloud intelligence to detect anomalous login behavior, impossible travel, and network-level indicators of compromised credential usage.
elements.toolTip: Deploys rule templates that leverage UEBA behavioral baselines alongside SpyCloud data.

elements.name: enableAdvancedThreatRules
elements.label: Enable Advanced Threat Detection Rules -- Sophisticated detection logic for infostealer malware infections, session hijacking via stolen cookies, lateral movement using compromised credentials, and multi-stage attack patterns.
elements.toolTip: Deploys advanced threat detection rule templates.

elements.name: enableExposureRules
elements.label: Enable Exposure Assessment Rules -- Evaluates organizational exposure levels based on breach frequency, credential volume, data recency, and asset criticality using SpyCloud exposure metrics.
elements.toolTip: Deploys exposure assessment rule templates.

elements.name: enableIDLinkRules
elements.label: Enable IDLink Correlation Rules -- Detects identity linkage patterns where multiple compromised accounts share devices, credentials, or personal information, indicating broader organizational risk.
elements.toolTip: Deploys IDLink correlation rule templates. Requires the IDLink data poller to be enabled.

elements.name: enableInvestigationsRules
elements.label: Enable Investigations Rules -- Provides detection templates for deep-dive investigative workflows including threat actor attribution, campaign tracking, and targeted credential theft analysis.
elements.toolTip: Deploys investigation-oriented rule templates. Requires the Investigations data poller.

elements.name: enableCAPRules
elements.label: Enable CAP Response Rules -- Detection and response templates for Compromised Application Protection events, including consumer account takeover detection, credential stuffing indicators, and automated response triggers.
elements.toolTip: Deploys CAP response rule templates. Requires the CAP data poller.

options.text: Select the automated response playbooks to deploy. Each playbook is deployed as a Logic App and requires appropriate API connections to be configured after deployment. Managed Identity permissions are assigned automatically where possible.

elements.name: enableForcePasswordReset
elements.label: Force Password Reset -- Triggers a password reset for user accounts with compromised credentials detected by SpyCloud. Requires Microsoft Graph API permissions.
elements.toolTip: Deploys the Force Password Reset playbook.

elements.name: enableRevokeSessions
elements.label: Revoke Sessions -- Invalidates all active sessions for compromised user accounts to terminate any unauthorized access. Requires Microsoft Graph API permissions.
elements.toolTip: Deploys the Revoke Sessions playbook.

elements.name: enableEnforceMFA
elements.label: Enforce MFA -- Applies multi-factor authentication requirements to compromised accounts via Conditional Access policy modification. Requires Entra ID P1 or P2.
elements.toolTip: Deploys the Enforce MFA playbook.

elements.name: enableBlockConditionalAccess
elements.label: Block via Conditional Access -- Adds compromised users to a designated Conditional Access group to restrict or block access based on your policy configuration. Requires Entra ID P1 or P2.
elements.toolTip: Deploys the Conditional Access blocking playbook.

elements.name: enableBlockFirewall
elements.label: Block via Firewall -- Pushes IP addresses and indicators from SpyCloud intelligence to your firewall or network security group rules for automated blocking.
elements.toolTip: Deploys the firewall blocking playbook.

elements.name: enableIsolateDevice
elements.label: Isolate Device via MDE -- Initiates network isolation of devices identified as infected by infostealer malware through Microsoft Defender for Endpoint. Requires MDE P2.
elements.toolTip: Deploys the MDE device isolation playbook.

elements.name: enableEnrichIncident
elements.label: Enrich Incident -- Automatically enriches Sentinel incidents with SpyCloud intelligence including breach history, exposure timeline, and related compromised assets for the affected identities.
elements.toolTip: Deploys the incident enrichment playbook.

elements.name: enableFullRemediation
elements.label: Full Remediation Orchestrator -- Coordinates a comprehensive response workflow combining password reset, session revocation, MFA enforcement, and device isolation based on incident severity and affected asset type.
elements.toolTip: Deploys the full remediation orchestrator playbook.

options.text: Provide the Entra ID group Object IDs used by Conditional Access playbooks. These groups should be pre-created in Entra ID and associated with your Conditional Access policies. Leave blank if not using Conditional Access automation.

elements.name: blockedGroupId
elements.label: Blocked Group ID
elements.toolTip: Entra ID group Object ID for users who should be fully blocked from sign-in.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a valid GUID or left empty.

elements.name: restrictedGroupId
elements.label: Restricted Group ID
elements.toolTip: Entra ID group Object ID for users with restricted access requiring additional authentication.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a valid GUID or left empty.

elements.name: monitoredGroupId
elements.label: Monitored Group ID
elements.toolTip: Entra ID group Object ID for users under enhanced monitoring without access restrictions.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a valid GUID or left empty.

elements.name: mdeIsolationType
elements.label: Device Isolation Type
elements.toolTip: Full isolation blocks all network connections except to the MDE service. Selective isolation allows limited connectivity such as Outlook and Teams.

elements.name: mdeTagName
elements.label: Device Tag Name
elements.toolTip: Tag applied to devices when isolated by SpyCloud automation for tracking and filtering in MDE.

constraints.validationMessage: Tag must be 1-64 characters using letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores.

options.text: Enrichment playbooks call the SpyCloud API in real-time when incidents are created, adding fresh breach context directly to incident comments. This is separate from the CCF pollers which do bulk data ingestion on a schedule. All enrichment playbooks use the Enterprise API key you provided in Step 1 — no additional key entry needed for base enrichment.

elements.name: enableEnrichmentPlaybooks
elements.label: Enable SpyCloud Enrichment Playbooks (recommended)
elements.toolTip: Deploy enrichment playbooks that call SpyCloud API at incident time for real-time entity-specific breach data. Uses the Enterprise API key provided above.

options.text: These playbooks use your Enterprise API key to enrich incidents with fresh breach data. Each triggers automatically when matching entities are found in SpyCloud incidents. No additional API keys needed.

elements.name: enableEnrichEmail
elements.label: Email Enrichment — Look up breach records by email address
elements.toolTip: Calls /breach/data/emails/{email} to get all exposures for an email. Returns: total exposures, severity, plaintext passwords, target domains, infection details. Most commonly triggered enrichment.

elements.name: enableEnrichDomain
elements.label: Domain Enrichment — Look up breach records by domain
elements.toolTip: Calls /breach/data/domains/{domain} to assess organizational exposure scope. Returns: total exposed users, severity breakdown, top exposed accounts, breach sources.

elements.name: enableEnrichIP
elements.label: IP Enrichment — Look up breach records by IP address
elements.toolTip: Calls /breach/data/ips/{ip} to find infected devices at an IP. Returns: devices, users, malware families, infection dates. Valuable for infostealer investigations.

elements.name: enableEnrichUsername
elements.label: Username Enrichment — Look up breach records by username
elements.toolTip: Calls /breach/data/usernames/{username} for non-email identifiers. Useful for service accounts or identifiers without email format.

elements.name: enableEnrichCatalog
elements.label: Catalog Enrichment — Enrich incidents with breach source context
elements.toolTip: Calls /breach/catalog/{source_id} to add 'where did this breach come from?' context. Returns: breach title, description, type, confidence, record count.

elements.name: enableFullInvestigation
elements.label: Full Investigation Orchestration — Chains multiple enrichments for Critical incidents
elements.toolTip: For High/Critical severity incidents only. Chains: email lookup → catalog enrichment → Compass device (if available) → SIP cookies (if available) → MDE correlation → Entra sign-in correlation. Makes 3-10 API calls depending on available licenses. Rate limited: max 1/minute, 50/day.

elements.name: sectionCompass
elements.label: Compass — Endpoint Threat Protection (Requires Compass License)

options.text: Compass provides post-infection visibility into what infostealers stole from devices — application credentials, session tokens, browser passwords, autofill data, and device fingerprints. This goes beyond the breach watchlist to show the complete blast radius of an infection. Requires a separate Compass API key from SpyCloud.

elements.name: enableEnrichCompass
elements.label: Enable Compass Enrichment Playbooks
elements.toolTip: Deploy Compass enrichment playbooks for consumer exposure data, application-level stolen credentials, and device forensics. Requires Compass API key below.

elements.name: compassApiKeyInput
elements.toolTip: SpyCloud Compass API key from portal.spycloud.com. Leave blank if you don't have a Compass license — pollers and enrichment for Compass will be skipped.

elements.name: sectionSip
elements.label: SIP — Session Identity Protection (Requires SIP License)

options.text: SIP detects stolen session cookies and authentication tokens that enable MFA bypass. When attackers have valid session cookies, they can impersonate users WITHOUT needing their password or MFA. This is the most critical data type for preventing session hijacking attacks.

elements.name: enableEnrichStolenCookies
elements.label: Enable SIP Stolen Cookie Enrichment
elements.toolTip: Calls /sip/cookies/emails/{email} to find stolen session cookies. Reports affected applications, cookie types, MFA bypass risk assessment. Requires SIP API key.

elements.name: sipApiKeyInput
elements.toolTip: SpyCloud SIP API key. May be the same as your Enterprise key depending on your license tier.

elements.name: sectionInvestigations
elements.label: Investigations — Deep Database Access (Requires Investigations License)

options.text: Investigations provides full SpyCloud database access for threat hunting and attribution. Unlike Enterprise (scoped to your watchlist), Investigations searches the ENTIRE database. Use for: threat actor attribution, supply chain risk, insider threat investigation. API rate limits are typically lower — use judiciously.

elements.name: enableEnrichInvestigation
elements.label: Enable Investigations Enrichment Playbooks
elements.toolTip: Deploy deep investigation playbooks. Makes full-database lookups by email, IP, and username. Rate limited due to higher API cost.

options.text: SpyCloud API has per-key rate limits (typically 2-10 QPS for Enterprise). The enrichment playbooks include built-in retry logic with exponential backoff. Set a daily limit below to prevent exceeding your quota. Default of 200 handles ~50-100 incidents/day.

elements.name: enrichmentDailyLimit
elements.label: Daily Enrichment API Call Limit
elements.toolTip: Maximum API calls per day across all enrichment playbooks. Prevents exceeding SpyCloud quota.

options.text: Configure notification channels and ITSM integrations. These are optional and can be configured or updated after deployment. Credentials are stored securely in Logic App API connections.

elements.name: enableEmail
elements.label: Enable email notifications for SpyCloud incidents and alerts.
elements.toolTip: Sends email notifications via Office 365 or SendGrid when incidents are created.

elements.name: notificationEmail
elements.label: Notification Email Address
elements.toolTip: Email address or distribution list for incident notifications.

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid email address.

elements.name: emailFrequency
elements.label: Email Frequency
elements.toolTip: Controls how frequently notification emails are sent.

elements.name: enableTeams
elements.label: Enable Microsoft Teams notifications for SpyCloud incidents.
elements.toolTip: Posts adaptive cards to a Teams channel when incidents are created.

elements.name: teamsWebhookUrl
elements.label: Teams Webhook URL
elements.toolTip: Incoming webhook URL for the Teams channel. Create one via Teams channel settings > Connectors > Incoming Webhook.

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid HTTPS URL.

elements.name: teamsChannelId
elements.label: Teams Channel ID (alternative to webhook)
elements.toolTip: The Teams channel ID for posting via Graph API. Use this if you prefer Graph API integration over webhooks.

elements.name: teamsGroupId
elements.label: Teams Group (Team) ID
elements.toolTip: The Entra ID group ID of the Team. Required when using Channel ID for Graph API integration.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a valid GUID or left empty.

elements.name: enableSlack
elements.label: Enable Slack notifications for SpyCloud incidents.
elements.toolTip: Posts formatted messages to a Slack channel via incoming webhook.

elements.name: slackWebhookUrl
elements.label: Slack Webhook URL *
elements.toolTip: Incoming webhook URL from your Slack workspace configuration.
elements.placeholder: Enter your Slack incoming webhook URL

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid Slack webhook URL.

elements.name: slackChannelName
elements.label: Slack Channel Name
elements.toolTip: Override the default channel configured in the webhook.

elements.name: slackBotDisplayName
elements.label: Bot Display Name
elements.toolTip: Display name for the bot posting messages in Slack.

elements.name: enableWebhook
elements.label: Enable generic webhook notifications for incident data forwarding.
elements.toolTip: Sends incident payloads to a custom webhook endpoint.

elements.name: webhookUrl
elements.label: Webhook URL *
elements.toolTip: HTTPS endpoint to receive incident notification payloads.

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid HTTPS URL.

elements.name: webhookCustomHeaders
elements.label: Custom Headers (JSON)
elements.toolTip: Optional JSON object of custom HTTP headers to include with webhook requests. Example: {"Authorization": "Bearer token123", "X-Source": "SpyCloud"}

elements.name: enableServiceNow
elements.label: Enable ServiceNow integration to create tickets for SpyCloud incidents.
elements.toolTip: Creates ServiceNow incidents or records when Sentinel incidents are generated.

elements.name: snowInstanceUrl
elements.label: ServiceNow Instance URL *
elements.toolTip: Your ServiceNow instance URL.

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid ServiceNow instance URL (e.g., https://contoso.service-now.com).

elements.name: snowAuthType
elements.label: Authentication Type
elements.toolTip: Authentication method for ServiceNow API access.

elements.name: snowUsername
elements.label: Username *
elements.toolTip: ServiceNow service account username with appropriate table permissions.

elements.name: snowPassword
elements.toolTip: ServiceNow service account password.

elements.name: snowTable
elements.label: Table Name
elements.toolTip: ServiceNow table to create records in.

elements.name: snowAssignmentGroup
elements.label: Assignment Group
elements.toolTip: ServiceNow assignment group for created tickets.

elements.name: enableJira
elements.label: Enable Jira integration to create issues for SpyCloud incidents.
elements.toolTip: Creates Jira issues when Sentinel incidents are generated.

elements.name: jiraInstanceUrl
elements.label: Jira Instance URL *
elements.toolTip: Your Jira Cloud or Server instance URL.

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid HTTPS URL.

elements.name: jiraEmail
elements.label: Jira Account Email *
elements.toolTip: Email address associated with the Jira service account.

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid email address.

elements.name: jiraApiToken
elements.toolTip: Jira API token generated from your Atlassian account settings.

elements.name: jiraProjectKey
elements.label: Project Key *
elements.toolTip: The Jira project key where issues will be created (e.g., SEC, SOC, SECURITY).

constraints.validationMessage: Project key must be 2-10 uppercase characters starting with a letter.

elements.name: jiraIssueType
elements.label: Issue Type
elements.toolTip: Jira issue type for created tickets.

elements.name: enableAzureDevOps
elements.label: Enable Azure DevOps integration to create work items for SpyCloud incidents.
elements.toolTip: Creates Azure DevOps work items when Sentinel incidents are generated.

elements.name: adoOrgUrl
elements.label: Organization URL *
elements.toolTip: Your Azure DevOps organization URL.

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid Azure DevOps organization URL (e.g., https://dev.azure.com/contoso).

elements.name: adoProject
elements.label: Project Name *
elements.toolTip: Azure DevOps project where work items will be created.

elements.name: adoWorkItemType
elements.label: Work Item Type
elements.toolTip: Type of work item to create in Azure DevOps.

elements.name: enableUEBA
elements.label: Enable User and Entity Behavior Analytics (UEBA) -- Activates behavioral profiling for user and entity accounts. UEBA baselines require approximately 14 days of data collection before anomaly detection becomes effective.
elements.toolTip: Enables UEBA in Microsoft Sentinel for behavioral analytics.

elements.name: enableAnomalyDetection
elements.label: Enable Anomaly Detection -- Activates built-in anomaly detection rules in Microsoft Sentinel for identifying unusual patterns in sign-in activity, data access, and network behavior.
elements.toolTip: Enables built-in anomaly detection rules.

elements.name: enableFusionRule
elements.label: Enable Fusion Rule -- Activates the Microsoft Sentinel Fusion ML-based rule for multi-stage attack detection. Note: Only one Fusion rule instance is supported per workspace. Do not enable if a Fusion rule already exists.
elements.toolTip: Deploys the Fusion advanced multi-stage attack detection rule. Only one instance per workspace is supported.

options.text: Only one Fusion rule instance is supported per Microsoft Sentinel workspace. If a Fusion rule already exists in your workspace, enabling this option may cause a deployment conflict. Verify your workspace does not already have an active Fusion rule before proceeding.

elements.name: enableMSICRules
elements.label: Enable Microsoft Security Incident Creation Rules -- Deploys rules that automatically create Sentinel incidents from alerts generated by Microsoft security products (Defender for Endpoint, Defender for Identity, Defender for Cloud Apps, etc.).
elements.toolTip: Deploys MSIC analytics rules for Microsoft security product alert integration.

options.text: Apply tags to all deployed resources for cost tracking, governance, and organizational management. These tags are applied uniformly to every resource created by this deployment.

elements.name: environmentTag
elements.label: Environment
elements.toolTip: Indicates the deployment environment for resource classification.

elements.name: ownerTag
elements.label: Owner
elements.toolTip: Name or email of the person or team responsible for this deployment.

elements.name: costCenterTag
elements.label: Cost Center
elements.toolTip: Cost center code for billing allocation of deployed resources.

options.text: Review your configuration before deploying SpyCloud for Microsoft Sentinel. After deployment, all analytics rules will be available as templates in Sentinel > Analytics > Rule Templates. Playbooks will be deployed as Logic Apps and may require API connection authorization. Use the navigation buttons to return to any previous step and modify your selections.

Pre-deployment requirements:

- A valid SpyCloud API key with appropriate tier access.
- Contributor or Owner role on the target resource group.
- Microsoft Sentinel enabled on the target Log Analytics workspace (or selected to create a new workspace).
- Microsoft.Insights, Microsoft.OperationalInsights, and Microsoft.Logic resource providers registered in the subscription.
- For Conditional Access playbooks: Entra ID P1 or P2 license and pre-created security groups.
- For MDE playbooks: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint P2 license.
- For ITSM integrations: Valid service account credentials with appropriate permissions.
